steps.title: Find the original y-intercept
steps.content: Set $x=0$ in the original equation: $$9(0)+10y=19.$$ Then $$10y=19,$$ so $$y=\frac{19}{10}.$$

steps.title: Apply the vertical shift
steps.content: Shifting a graph up $6$ units adds $6$ to every $y$-value, including the $y$-intercept. The new intercept is $$\frac{19}{10}+6=\frac{19}{10}+\frac{60}{10}=\frac{79}{10}.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The resulting graph has $y$-intercept $(0,\frac{79}{10})$, which matches choice A.
